name: Google Gemini API
what_it_does: Google Gemini API provides access to Google's multimodal large language models (Gemini Pro, Flash, Ultra) for text generation, vision understanding, code generation, long-context reasoning, and function calling.
use_cases: Processing long documents (up to 2M token context window in Gemini 1.5 Pro) for summarization, extraction, or analysis tasks that exceed other models' context limits
Multimodal agents that need to analyze images, PDFs, audio, or video alongside text — Gemini's native multimodal capabilities avoid separate vision API calls
Function calling and structured JSON output generation for agents that orchestrate tool use with reliable schema adherence
Code generation, review, and debugging tasks using Gemini's strong coding capabilities with inline code execution via Code Execution tool
High-throughput, cost-sensitive agent workflows using Gemini Flash — optimized for speed and low cost while maintaining strong reasoning for routine tasks
not_for: Agents that require OpenAI-compatible endpoints natively — Gemini uses a different API shape and SDK; migration from OpenAI requires code changes
Real-time voice conversation requiring sub-200ms turn latency — Gemini's streaming API is fast but not optimized for telephony-grade latency
Enterprise deployments requiring FedRAMP High or HIPAA BAAs on the Gemini API directly — use Vertex AI instead for regulated workloads
best_when: You need a capable multimodal LLM with an industry-leading context window, competitive pricing on Flash models, and native function calling — especially when your stack is already Google Cloud or you want a credible OpenAI alternative.
avoid_when: Your team has significant investment in OpenAI's API ecosystem (Assistants, fine-tuned models, Batch API) and migration costs outweigh Gemini's advantages for your specific use case.

auth.notes: Gemini API (via Google AI Studio) uses API keys — simplest option for development. Production deployments should use Vertex AI with service accounts or workload identity federation. API keys are project-scoped but not resource-scoped — a single key can access all Gemini models in the project. Google AI Studio API keys have no built-in IP restriction; use Vertex AI for network-restricted access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Gemini 1.5 Flash: 15 RPM, 1M TPM, 1,500 RPD free. Gemini 1.5 Pro: 2 RPM, 32K TPD free. Free tier available via Google AI Studio only (not Vertex AI).
pricing.paid_tiers: Gemini 1.5 Flash: $0.075/M input tokens (up to 128K), $0.30/M output tokens. Gemini 1.5 Pro: $1.25/M input tokens (up to 128K), $5.00/M output tokens. Context caching discounts available for repeated long contexts. Gemini 2.0 Flash: $0.10/M input, $0.40/M output.
pricing.notes: Free tier rate limits are restrictive for production use. Paid tier pricing is competitive with GPT-4o-mini equivalents for Flash. Long-context inputs (>128K tokens) are billed at 2.5x the base rate for Pro. Batch API available for 50% discount on async workloads.

performance.rate_limits: Free tier: 15 RPM for Flash, 2 RPM for Pro. Paid tier: 1,000 RPM for Flash, 360 RPM for Pro (scalable via quota increase). Token per minute limits apply separately. Quota increases available through Google Cloud Console.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are well-structured with status codes, error.code, and error.message. Rate limit errors (429) include quota type information. Safety filter blocks return FINISH_REASON: SAFETY with category and probability fields — helpful for debugging content policy issues. Function call validation errors are specific about which parameter failed schema validation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Generation requests are not idempotent by design — the same prompt produces different outputs. No request deduplication mechanism. For deterministic output, set temperature=0 but results may still vary due to model updates.
known_agent_gotchas: Safety filters can block agent-generated prompts that contain security-related content, vulnerability descriptions, or role-play scenarios — agents must handle FINISH_REASON: SAFETY gracefully and implement fallback logic

Function calling requires the model to return a FunctionCall part, which the agent must execute and return as a FunctionResponse part in the next turn — the multi-turn tool use pattern differs from OpenAI's tool_calls structure

The 2M token context window of Gemini 1.5 Pro is real but slow and expensive — context windows above 128K are billed at 2.5x and latency increases significantly; do not use full context as a substitute for retrieval
Streaming responses deliver chunks as GenerateContentResponse objects, not raw token deltas — each chunk may contain multiple candidates and finish reasons that must be accumulated correctly

Google AI Studio API keys and Vertex AI service account credentials use completely different SDKs (google-generativeai vs google-cloud-aiplatform) — mixing projects using both creates dependency and config management complexity

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys for Google AI Studio have no IP restriction or scope limitation — a leaked key grants full model access. Production use should migrate to Vertex AI with service accounts and Workload Identity Federation. Prompt data is not used for model training when accessed via API (vs. AI Studio chat UI). CSAM and harmful content detection is built into the safety filter layer.
